title: pyrus pyraster var. achras
intro: Pyrus pyraster var. achras, also known as the wild pear or mountain pear, is a variety of wild pear tree native to Europe and Western Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pyrus pyraster var. achras include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the bark can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical application.
